Article.ArticleTitle: A Robust Closed-Tube Method for Resolving tp53M214K Genotypes.

Abstract.AbstractText: The tp53M214K zebrafish mutant is a versatile platform with which to model a diverse spectrum of human diseases. However, currently available genotyping methods for this mutant require lengthy hands-on processes such as restriction digests and outsourced Sanger sequencing. To address this deficiency, we leveraged high-resolution melting analysis technology in conjunction with a parallel, in-tandem wild-type spike-in approach to develop a robust genotyping protocol capable of discriminating tp53M214K zygosity. In this study, we describe our method in detail. We anticipate that our genotyping protocol will benefit researchers utilizing the tp53M214K zebrafish mutant by offering reliable results with a shorter turnaround time, lower personnel involvement, and higher throughput than traditional methods, thereby decreasing the burden of genotyping and maximizing research efficiency.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Center for Childhood Cancer Research, The Abigail Wexner Research Institute, Nationwide Children's Hospital, Columbus, Ohio, USA.
